name: The Golf Station
about: The Golf Station is an indoor golf simulator venue located in Granite City, Illinois, operating out of a renovated historic Route 66 gas station. It provides year-round golf access through a state-of-the-art simulator, catering to players for practice, play, and group events. The facility features a full bar and is designed for casual play, practice sessions, and social gatherings with friends and family. Their location and hours are structured to accommodate both weekday and evening play, with extended hours on weekends and special hours on Thursdays and Fridays.
why_golfers_like_it: Year-round indoor golf access regardless of weather
Convenient booking system for tee times
Social and party atmosphere suitable for various events
Located in a unique historic Route 66 setting

food_and_drink.notes: BYOB (Bring Your Own Beverages) is not explicitly mentioned, but a full bar is available on-site
Full bar available with accepted payment methods including Cash App, Apple Pay, Visa, Mastercard, American Express, Discover, and JCB

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: The venue does not explicitly state whether club rentals are available. It is recommended to bring your own clubs or contact the venue directly for more information.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drinks?
faq.answer: The venue has a full bar on-site, but it does not explicitly mention if outside food and drinks are allowed. It is best to check with the venue directly regarding their policy on outside food and beverages.

faq.question: How long does it typically take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: The website does not specify the exact time required to play 18 holes. However, it is common for simulator venues to allow approximately 1-2 hours for a full 18-hole game, depending on the number of players and pace of play.

faq.question: Is the venue kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the venue appears to be family-friendly, as it mentions being 'Perfect for practice, parties and fun with friends and family,' which suggests it accommodates children and families.
